overview: A single-component microservice architecture consisting of a lightweight HTTP API that returns a static greeting message. The service exposes a REST endpoint accessible over HTTP for connectivity testing and health verification. No external dependencies or data persistence required.
requirements: The API must respond with 'Hello, World!' message when called
The API must return HTTP 200 status code for successful requests
The API must be accessible over HTTP protocol
The API must support standard HTTP GET requests
The API must be testable using common tools like curl and web browsers
The API must include a /health endpoint for readiness checks

components.name: hello-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello World API
  description: A simple API that returns a greeting message for connectivity testing
  version: 1.0.0
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
    description: Local development server
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Get hello world message
      description: Returns a simple greeting message to confirm the service is running
      operationId: getHello
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Successful response
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  message:
                    type: string
                    example: Hello, World!
                required:
                  - message
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      description: Returns the health status of the service
      operationId: healthCheck
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
                required:
                  - status
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a minimal HTTP API service in Go using the standard net/http package. The service must listen on port 9090. Create two endpoints: (1) GET / that returns JSON {"message":"Hello, World!"} with status 200, and (2) GET /health that returns JSON {"status":"ok"} with status 200. Use json.Marshal or json.NewEncoder to properly serialize responses. Set Content-Type header to application/json for all responses. Handle errors gracefully and log any issues to stdout. The main.go file should be in the root of /hello-api directory. Use http.HandleFunc to register routes and http.ListenAndServe to start the server. Ensure the server starts cleanly and logs the listening port. Create a Dockerfile with multi-stage build: first stage uses golang:1.21 to build the binary, second stage uses alpine:latest as runtime with the compiled binary. Expose port 9090 in the Dockerfile. The service should have no external dependencies beyond the Go standard library. Include proper error handling for JSON marshaling failures. Log all incoming requests with method and path for observability.
